name: gmrepo

relevance.justification: GMrepo v3 is a near-perfect match for Demographic-Microbiome ingestion. It provides: (1) species-level microbiome abundance data from both 16S rRNA and WGS pipelines (726 species, 573 genera via SILVA 138 / MetaPhlAn4), (2) phenotype/disease data for 302 distinct phenotypes using standardized MeSH terminology, (3) per-sample demographic metadata including age, gender, BMI, and geography. The database is actively maintained (v3 published NAR 2026, D1 issue) with 118,965 samples across 890 projects. Data is structured/tabular (TSV bulk downloads, SQL dump). Standard identifiers used: NCBI Taxonomy IDs, MeSH terms. Minor deduction: only ~30% of samples have gender annotation, and ethnicity is not explicitly captured as a field.

novelty.justification: GMrepo v3 is not currently in any BioThings API. It represents a distinct, curated entity type (human gut microbiome runs linked to phenotypes and demographics) not covered by MyGene, MyDisease, MyChem, or MyVariant. Key novel contributions: (1) 118,965 curated and QC-passed samples with processed abundance profiles, (2) 1,299 cross-dataset microbial biomarkers (726 species, 573 genera) validated across 167 phenotype pairs, (3) co-occurrence data by phenotype, (4) cross-dataset meta-analysis results. HumanMetagenomeDB (already evaluated, NEEDS_REVIEW) has overlapping raw-data scope but GMrepo adds curated QC, re-processed abundances, and biomarker analysis on top.

openness.justification: All bulk data files are publicly downloadable via direct URL at https://gmrepo.humangut.info/Downloads/SQLDumps/ — confirmed by successful binary download of projects.txt.gz with no login redirect. Includes full SQL dump, per-sample metadata, species abundance TSVs, and co-occurrence tables. RESTful API also available (no auth required per documentation). License is Creative Commons Attribution Non-Commercial (CC-BY-NC), which is compatible with BioThings ingestion at Scripps Research (non-profit/academic). Minor risk: downstream commercial API consumers could conflict with NC clause.

evidence.scope: Human gut microbiome samples with per-sample demographics (age, gender, BMI) and disease phenotypes (302 phenotypes via MeSH). Abundance profiles at species and genus levels. Cross-dataset microbial biomarker associations. Run-level QC status. Taxon co-occurrence by phenotype.
evidence.overlap: Partially overlaps with HumanMetagenomeDB in raw sample scope, but GMrepo v3 uniquely provides: re-processed and QC-filtered abundance profiles, standardized MeSH phenotype annotations, and cross-dataset biomarker analysis results. No overlap with any current BioThings API source.
evidence.demographic_coverage_note: ~30% of samples have gender annotation; ethnicity not a captured field

api_info.notes: RESTful API with example code in R, Perl, and Python via GitHub. Graphical query builder also available on site. API likely query-access only; bulk download preferred for full ingestion.

risks: CC-BY-NC license: downstream commercial consumers of BioThings APIs may technically violate the NC clause — recommend confirming acceptable use with Chen lab maintainers
~30% gender annotation rate means demographic completeness is partial across samples
Ethnicity not a captured metadata field — limits full demographic-KG alignment
species_abundance.txt.gz may be large; confirm file size before ingestion planning
v2 still live at gmrepo2022.humangut.info — ensure plugin targets v3 endpoint canonical URLs
next_actions: Run site inspection to verify all download URLs and file sizes
Confirm CC-BY-NC is acceptable for BioThings API redistribution with legal/maintainers
Proceed to plugin generation targeting species_abundance.txt.gz + sample_to_run_info.txt.gz + sample_to_disease_info.txt.gz as core files
